hypothesis: For 1003 deterministically generated binary traces of length 64 forced through six CUDA chunks, the CUDA sideways reconstruction returns all 63 reconstructed initial-left bits per trace exactly as an independent CPU oracle; timings expose chunk and launch overhead for this exact workload.
interpretation: All 63189 finite output bytes matched the independent CPU oracle in the warm-up and every measured run. Forced chunking raised CUDA median end-to-end time to 9.676108 ms versus 0.996188 ms for the CPU oracle; the 7.441408 ms median kernel total demonstrates that chunk and launch overhead matters for this small workload.
limitations: The test covers 1003 finite pseudorandom traces through reconstruction horizon 63; it does not establish compatibility or incompatibility of any infinite or eventually periodic center trace.
The forced six-chunk configuration was selected to exercise chunking and uneven grids, not because memory pressure required six chunks on this GPU.
The timing result applies only to this exact chunk cap, block size, workload, and host; it is not a general CPU or CUDA performance claim.
The CPU oracle is an independent single-threaded O(horizon)-workspace implementation of the same finite mapping.
One CPU warm-up and one GPU warm-up preceded only three measured repetitions; frequency scaling and unrelated host activity were not disabled.
Sideways reconstruction performs quadratic work in the finite horizon and supplies no asymptotic shortcut for c_n.
The 39 C benchmark-session readings and 40 C final idle reading are coarse snapshots, not a per-repetition thermal trace; no clock, voltage, fan, power, or thermal control was changed.
Compute Sanitizer 2026.2.1 could not initialize its WSL/WDDM debugger interface, so this milestone has passing functional tests but no successful Compute Sanitizer run.

parameters.trace_generation: xorshift with shifts 13, 7, 17; each low bit is emitted

proof_scope: Exact finite equality of all 1003*63 reconstructed bytes between the independent CPU oracle and CUDA across six chunks in the warm-up and each of three measured runs; all performance conclusions are empirical and workload-specific.
question: problem1

software.algorithm: one CUDA thread per trace; finite Rule 30 left-permutive sideways reconstruction with three horizon-plus-two-byte work buffers per candidate
